issues.title: add deepseek chat model support
issues.body:
Please do a quick search on GitHub issues first, the feature you are about to request might have already been requested.

**Expected Behavior**
org.springframework.ai.chat.messages.Message  add reasoning_content

comments.body:
Thank you for opening this issue, @walle1027.  

There is already a PR open to resolve this: [#2192](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/2192).  

Additionally, there is another issue [#2283](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2283) with the same expectation.
You can also use Deepseek models via Ollama.
I am closing this as there are several other issues on the same topic.  Search for remaining deepseek issues and there are some comments on how we will proceed to handle supporting deepseek.

issues.title: Flux 模式下大模型生成toolCall 错误
issues.body:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3c3bdb81-ff5e-4f60-b4d0-9f73251c9ce6)

版本 1.0.0-M6 ,使用call()方法测试无问题，大模型生产的toolCall正确，但是stream()方法生成的就有问题，如图。


Flux<String> content = ChatClient.builder(chatModel)
                .build()
                .prompt()
                .tools(toolCallbackProvider.getToolCallbacks())
                .user("数据库下有什么表？每个表的schema怎么定义的？以表格形式呈现。数据库配置 ：host:127.0.0.1 ,user:root,password:123456,database:ai")
                .stream()
                .content();

以上代码生成toolCall有问题

String content = ChatClient.builder(chatModel)
                .build()
                .prompt()
                .tools(toolCallbackProvider.getToolCallbacks())
                .user("列举三条百度热榜")
                .call()
                .content();
以上代码生成的toolCall无问题

comments.body: 同样的问题，请问怎么解决的

> 同样的问题，请问怎么解决的

@wsldl123292 这个问题已经修复了，现在还没有发布里程碑版本，你可以试试这个版本1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
@wsldl123292 确实，刚才试了下，你更新到最新的snapshot，已经解决了这个问题。

issues.title: Multiple SSE connections issue and SYNC mode startup failure in McpClientAutoConfiguration
issues.body:
In the current implementation of `McpClientAutoConfiguration`, there are two critical issues:

1. When using `type: ASYNC`, both SSE implementations (WebFlux and HttpClient) attempt to establish connections to the same server endpoint simultaneously. This occurs because the auto-configuration allows both transports to be registered:

java
@AutoConfiguration(after = {
StdioTransportAutoConfiguration.class,
SseHttpClientTransportAutoConfiguration.class,
SseWebFluxTransportAutoConfiguration.class
})


This results in:
- Duplicate SSE connections to the same endpoint
- Unnecessary resource consumption
- Potential message duplication

2. When using `type: SYNC`, the application fails to start. This appears to be related to the order of auto-configuration initialization specified in the `@AutoConfiguration(after = {...})` annotation.

Example configuration where these issues occur:

yaml
spring:
ai:
mcp:
client:
type: ASYNC # or SYNC
server:
endpoint: "http://localhost:8080/"


Questions:
1. Why are both SSE implementations allowed to register and connect simultaneously?
2. Could we implement a mechanism to ensure only one SSE implementation is active at a time?
3. What is the recommended approach for handling these transport implementations to avoid duplicate connections?
4. Why does SYNC mode fail to start, and what is the proper way to handle the auto-configuration order?

Environment:
- Spring AI version: [1.0.0-M6]
- Spring Boot version: [3.4.3]
- Java version: [JDK 17]

comments.body:
It seems that this issue has been duplicated.
https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2486

This issue can be closed.
